questions.question: Explain all the methods of calculating mean of grouped data.
questions.answer: Mean of grouped data can be calculated using direct method, assumed mean method and step deviation method.

questions.question: Explain the construction and interpretation of ogive.
questions.answer: Ogive is constructed using cumulative frequencies and helps in locating median and quartiles.

questions.question: Explain median of grouped data with formula.
questions.answer: Median is calculated using median class and cumulative frequency formula.

questions.question: Explain mode of grouped data with formula.
questions.answer: Mode is calculated using modal class and neighbouring frequencies.

questions.question: Explain the differences between histogram and bar diagram.
questions.answer: Histogram represents continuous data while bar diagram represents discrete data.

questions.question: Explain frequency density and its necessity.
questions.answer: Frequency density is necessary when class intervals are unequal in histograms.

questions.question: Explain the complete process of statistical investigation.
questions.answer: Statistical investigation includes collection, classification, presentation, analysis and interpretation.

questions.question: Explain classification and tabulation of data with advantages.
questions.answer: Classification and tabulation simplify large data and improve clarity.

questions.question: Explain measures of central tendency and their significance.
questions.answer: Mean, median and mode summarise data using single representative values.

questions.question: Explain importance and limitations of statistics.
questions.answer: Statistics is important for decision making but depends on quality of data.

questions.question: Explain cumulative frequency curves and their types.
questions.answer: Cumulative frequency curves include less-than and more-than ogives.

questions.question: Explain bivariate data with examples.
questions.answer: Bivariate data involves two related variables such as height and weight.

questions.question: Explain data summarisation techniques.
questions.answer: Data summarisation includes averages, tables and graphs.

questions.question: Explain frequency curve and its significance.
questions.answer: Frequency curve gives a smooth representation of frequency distribution.

questions.question: Explain statistical error and its sources.
questions.answer: Statistical error arises due to sampling and measurement errors.

questions.question: Explain population census and sample survey.
questions.answer: Census studies entire population while sample survey studies a part.

questions.question: Explain the limitations of mean.
questions.answer: Mean is affected by extreme values and may not represent skewed data.

questions.question: Explain advantages and disadvantages of median.
questions.answer: Median is not affected by extreme values but lacks algebraic use.

questions.question: Explain advantages and disadvantages of mode.
questions.answer: Mode shows most common value but may not be unique.

questions.question: Explain the role of statistics in economics.
questions.answer: Statistics helps in analysis of production, consumption and income.

questions.question: Explain the role of statistics in business.
questions.answer: Statistics aids in forecasting, quality control and decision making.

questions.question: Explain the role of statistics in social sciences.
questions.answer: Statistics helps in analysing social data and trends.

questions.question: Explain interpretation and inference in statistics.
questions.answer: Interpretation explains data while inference draws conclusions about population.

questions.question: Explain importance of accurate data collection.
questions.answer: Accurate data ensures reliability and correctness of statistical results.

questions.question: Explain ethical issues in statistics.
questions.answer: Ethical issues include data manipulation, bias and misrepresentation.
